key_metrics_output: plastic quantification, particle identification, regulatory compliance, QA/QC metrics, data comparability
abstract: This article examines regulatory measures and methodological challenges for microplastics analysis in soils across agricultural, roadside, urban, natural, and forest environments. It critically reviews existing legal regulations and regional best-practice examples, highlighting how methodological discrepancies hinder data comparability and risk assessments. The paper provides a QA/QC-focused framework to improve reliability and comparability of plastic analysis in soil, addressing contamination risks and analytical standardization to support method harmonization and inform soil protection strategies.

key_metrics_output: identification of plastic entry routes and pollution hotspots
reliability and comparability of plastic analysis
